hero.title: Real Hospitality Results From The Anchor
hero.subtitle: Measured outcomes from practical, action-first changes. No vanity metrics, just numbers that matter.

caseStudies.title: Food Sales Stuck on Flat?
caseStudies.subtitle: "I'm busy, but the food side just won't grow"
problem: Food revenue had plateaued and wasn't pulling its weight
Outdated menu with descriptions that didn't sell
Pricing set by gut feel, with no strategy behind it
Food treated as a sideline rather than a profit centre
failed: Pushing prices up without improving quality — customers noticed
Switching to cheaper ingredients — quality dropped
solution: Partnered with commercial suppliers (Barrel And Stone, Brakes, Bidfood)
Used AI to analyse menu psychology and rewrite descriptions with sensory language
Built strategic pricing and deliberate menu positioning
Introduced stone-baked pizzas as a high-margin hero product
Regular AI-powered menu updates based on seasonal ingredients and costs

results.value: Higher-margin dishes now outselling the rest

learnings: Quality improvements justify higher prices
Sensory menu language lifts high-margin choices
A high-margin hero product can transform food sales
Consistent supplier relationships improve both cost and quality
caseStudies.quote: We stopped treating food as a necessary evil and turned it into a real profit driver. In three months, food revenue grew by 98% — the menu rewrite alone shifted what people ordered.

caseStudies.title: Tables Sitting Empty?
caseStudies.subtitle: "Quiet sessions that barely cover the staff"
problem: Empty tables on the sessions that should pay the bills
No real reason for people to book ahead
Little visibility for what was actually on
Bookings left entirely to chance
failed: Random discounts — cheapened the offer without filling tables
Changing nights around — confused the regulars
Bigger giveaways — ate into margin without moving numbers
solution: AI-planned events and offers that gave people a reason to come
A clear, direct booking journey that turned interest into confirmed tables
Promotion built to drive bookings, not just awareness
Automated reminders that kept booked tables showing up
learnings: People book when there's a clear reason and an easy way to do it
Promotion has to point at an action, not just impressions
Reminders protect the bookings you've already won
Consistency is what turns a one-off visit into a habit
caseStudies.quote: Our quiet sessions went from dead to dependable. Once booking was easy and there was a reason to come, table bookings climbed 403% — and the room finally felt alive.

caseStudies.title: Invisible When People Search?
caseStudies.subtitle: "We're online, but nobody finds us"
problem: Barely showing up when locals searched for somewhere to go
A website that just sat there like a digital brochure
Low local awareness despite the effort
No clear path from being found to getting booked
failed: Posting more often — still invisible in search
Generic SEO tweaks — no commercial focus
Hiring help that didn't understand pub life
solution: Launched an AI-optimised, search-led website (the-anchor.pub) in August 2025
Built pages around what locals actually search for
Strengthened local visibility through Google Business Profile and reviews
Designed conversion-led journeys from search straight to booking

results.value: Showing up for what locals search

timeInvestment: Initial setup plus light ongoing optimisation
learnings: Being found beats posting more
A website should be built to be found, not just to look good
Local search intent is where the bookings hide
Visibility only matters when it leads to an action
caseStudies.quote: We went from invisible to unmissable. Google Search visibility is up 828%, and the people finding us are walking through the door — not just scrolling past.

caseStudies.title: Losing Covers to No-Shows?
caseStudies.subtitle: "Empty tables I'd turned other people away for"
problem: No-shows leaving prepped tables empty
Unpredictable covers making prep a guessing game
Walk-ins turned away for bookings that never arrived
Staff stressed by not knowing what to expect
failed: Guessing from the weather — still inaccurate
Cutting the menu back — lost variety
Overbooking to compensate — made the chaos worse
solution: Built an easy online booking flow with a small deposit
Automated reminders ahead of every booking
Linked bookings to kitchen prep so the team knew exactly what to expect
learnings: A small deposit creates commitment without scaring people off
Reminders are the cheapest way to protect a booking
Predictable demand makes buying and prep far easier
Confident staff deliver a better service
caseStudies.quote: No-shows used to wreck our best sessions. A simple deposit and reminder system cut them by 89% — now the tables we hold are tables that actually turn up.

caseStudies.title: Private Hire Barely Moving?
caseStudies.subtitle: "We've got the space, but the diary's empty"
problem: Function space sitting empty most of the year
A trickle of private bookings and no system to grow them
Enquiries slipping through the cracks
No clear offer to make the space worth booking
failed: Generic packages — nothing to get excited about
Random supplier tie-ups — no coherent experience
Pricing too low — attracted the wrong enquiries
solution: Positioned private hire with premium, curated experiences
Made the space easy to find for searches like private hire nearby
Built a smooth enquiry-to-booking journey that converted interest
Partnered with quality local suppliers and priced for the experience

results.value: Filling sessions that used to sit empty

learnings: A clear, premium offer makes the space worth booking
Most private hire starts with a local search
Enquiries convert when the journey is easy
Scarcity and quality let you price with confidence
caseStudies.quote: Our function space went from an afterthought to a real revenue line. Private hire bookings grew 567% once we made the space easy to find, easy to enquire about, and worth choosing.
